rules.name: Bulleted Output Format

check.description: More than 30% of non-empty lines are prose (not bullets, headings, tables, or code blocks)

rules.corrective_guidance: Your response must use bullet-point formatting. Rewrite all prose paragraphs as bulleted lists. Each point should be a single bullet starting with - or *. Headings, tables, and code blocks are allowed.

rules.name: Sequential Domain Confirmation

check.description: Detects when multiple domain confirmations (Accept/Amend prompts) appear in a single message

rules.corrective_guidance: You must present domain confirmations sequentially -- one domain per message. Present only the current domain's confirmation (Accept/Amend). Do not include confirmations for other domains in this message.

rules.name: Elicitation Before Analysis

check.description: Detects analysis completion declarations without any elicitation questions

rules.corrective_guidance: You must ask at least one elicitation question before declaring analysis complete. Open with a question to the user about their requirements, context, or preferences. Do not present a finished analysis without first engaging the user in conversation.

rules.name: Template Section Order Enforcement

check.description: Validates that domain confirmation responses follow the section order defined in the corresponding .isdlc/config/templates/*.template.json file. For requirements: functional_requirements → assumptions_and_inferences → non_functional_requirements → out_of_scope → prioritization. For architecture: architecture_options → selected_architecture → technology_decisions → integration_architecture → assumptions_and_inferences. For design: module_overview → module_design → changes_to_existing → wiring_summary → assumptions_and_inferences. For tasks: the 4-column traceability table (FR | Requirement | Design / Blast Radius | Related Tasks) followed by an Assumptions and Inferences section.

rules.corrective_guidance: Your confirmation response does not follow the template section order. Read the template at .isdlc/config/templates/{domain}.template.json and re-emit your response with sections in EXACTLY the order specified by section_order. Do not add extra sections, do not reorder, do not rename. For the Tasks confirmation, you MUST use the 4-column traceability table format from traceability.template.json (FR | Requirement | Design / Blast Radius | Related Tasks) with narrative + ACs in the Requirement column and narrative + file paths in the Design column, then add the required Assumptions and Inferences section.
